summary: Large item-system resplit PR that split the former broad src/melee/it/it_2725.c region into multiple item translation units, updated split/symbol metadata, configure.py object matching status, headers, includes, and many dependent item/fighter/stage references. The PR body says the split was derived iteratively with Claude/Codex from repeated floats/strings plus stronger filename or string evidence for several units. All new units were described as matching and linked except itmaplib; bot report shows broad match/link/data gains alongside expected broken matches from moving symbols out of it_2725.

key_files.role: Resplit the it_2725 address range into it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako with new text/data/sdata/sdata2 ranges.
Adjusted symbol names, scopes, sizes, and local labels around the resplit item data sections.
Changed the build object list so it_2725 and most new split units are Matching, while itmaplib remains NonMatching.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:All of these are matching and linked except itmaplib

key_files.role: Former monolithic item TU reduced substantially after code was moved into newly split item TUs.
New split unit for item hitbox setup, scaling, state toggles, and hitbox-derived collision queries per PR body.
New split unit for item map collision behavior; PR body cites __assert("itmaplib.c", ...) as filename evidence and says this unit is the exception that is not matching/linked.
New split unit for item material/render manipulation; PR body cites struct it_MObjInfo it_803F1F90 object as filename/source clustering evidence.
New split unit for item effect archive setup and randomized sync-effect spawning helpers.
New split unit for item animation-command handlers taking CommandInfo*; PR body cites __assert("itanimlist.c", ...) as filename evidence.
New split unit aggregating Pokémon, character-specific, monster, and stage-item tables plus shared helpers; the PR explicitly notes low confidence in a proposed semantic name.
New split unit for Zako/enemy-style items and grzakogenerator use; PR body cites the "can t init zako pos" string as filename inference evidence.
Header reduced/reorganized as declarations moved to new split headers.
New header for declarations moved to the it_279C split.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:include changes from it_2725.h to it_279C.h

key_files.role: New header for itmaplib split declarations.
New header for itzako split declarations, used by grzakogenerator/ground include updates.
Renamed or moved item-related structs/types, including evidence of it_279D_ItemVars to itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars.

what_changed: Changed 152 files with 9610 additions and 9954 deletions according to PR metadata.
Resplit the former it_2725.c range into eight item TUs: it_2725, ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako.
Added new split headers for several new item TUs and updated dependent includes in fighter, ground, and item files.
Updated configure.py so it_2725, ithitbox, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako are Matching objects, while itmaplib is NonMatching.
Updated symbols.txt around the resplit area, including scope changes from global to local for several strings/floats, local @ labels, and sbss size corrections.
Removed src/melee/it/it_3F14.c content according to changed-file metadata, with replacement/migration details not fully visible in the loaded diff excerpt.
Renamed some data/types to more semantic item-specific names visible in the excerpt, including it_27CF_DatAttrs to itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs and it_279D_ItemVars to itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars.

Bot report recorded +0.11% matched code, +0.90% linked code, +1.40% matched data, +1.42% linked data, 157 new matches, and expected broken matches under the old it_2725 unit after moving functions into new units.

smart_moves.text: The PR did not blindly rename every unclear split: it_2725 stayed address-style because its purpose was still unclear, and it_279C stayed address-style because the suggested semantic name was low confidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Codex suggested itspecial, but with low confidence

smart_moves.text: The resplit used multiple forms of TU-boundary evidence: repeated floats/strings as initial clustering signals, assert filenames for itmaplib and itanimlist, an object name for itmaterial, and a diagnostic string for itzako.
The configure.py status accurately reflects partial progress rather than overclaiming: itmaplib is left NonMatching despite being split out, matching the PR body's caveat.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:All of these are matching and linked except itmaplib

smart_moves.text: Includes were redirected to more specific split headers where call sites no longer needed the broad it_2725.h surface.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large legacy catch-all TUs, repeated float/string clusters can be useful initial split hints, but stronger evidence such as assert filenames, object names, diagnostic strings, and callsite/header usage should be recorded per new TU.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:iteratively split it_2725 into a bunch of TUs based on the presence of repeated floats/strings
human_pr_text:Filename inferred from a "can t init zako pos" string

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a resplit moves matched functions out of an old TU, report regressions in the old unit may be expected and should be interpreted together with new matches in the destination units.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:decomp-dev bot 132 broken matches in main/melee/it/it_2725

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not promote low-confidence AI-suggested semantic TU names when the PR evidence says purpose is unclear; keeping address-style names can be the more conservative, reviewable choice.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Codex suggested itspecial, but with low confidence

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Keep unclear split units address-style, such as it_2725 and it_279C, when semantic names are only AI suggestions or low confidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Codex suggested itspecial, but with low confidence

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Use subsystem-specific semantic names when supported by local role evidence, such as ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, and itzako.
Prefer more item-specific type names when the item role is evident, as shown by it_27CF_DatAttrs to itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs and it_279D_ItemVars to itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars in the excerpt.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split configuration ranges exactly along observed text/data/sdata/sdata2 boundaries for the new TUs, then update symbol metadata scopes and local labels to match the new ownership.
Use configure.py object status to encode per-TU match state after a split, rather than treating the entire former monolith as one NonMatching object.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:All of these are matching and linked except itmaplib

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use assert filename strings and other embedded strings as split-boundary or TU-identity evidence.
When a split creates many apparent old-unit broken matches, compare the destination-unit new matches and aggregate progress before concluding regression.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.body: This is really useful, thank you.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or rename itmaplib after PR 2546.
Search for later references to it_279C semantic naming, itspecial, or itcommon.
Search for PRs touching __assert("itmaplib.c") or __assert("itanimlist.c") as split evidence.
Search for subsequent item resplits that use repeated floats/strings as initial clustering evidence.
Search for changes around itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs and itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars after this resplit.
Search for source-corpus/path-facts entries documenting item TU split ranges around 0x80272560-0x8027CF00.

notes: The loaded diff is truncated and only excerpts selected files, so fine-grained per-function move details and the full list of 152 changed files are not completely visible in loaded evidence.
The PR body explicitly says Claude and Codex helped derive the split, but author intent beyond the written body is not inferred.
The PR body claims all split TUs except itmaplib are matching and linked; this is supported by configure.py status and bot report, but no local build/objdiff logs are loaded.
Some changed-file metadata in metadata_json and changed_files.jsonl differ in ordering and visible completeness due to truncation; statements about counts use metadata_json totals.
Review feedback is limited to one approving review with a short positive comment; no inline review comments are present.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Conservative naming for AI-assisted TU resplits
accepted_candidate_records.text: When AI-assisted split suggestions produce semantic names but the PR evidence says purpose is unclear or confidence is low, keep address-style TU names until stronger source evidence appears.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:it_2725 purpose unclear / Codex suggested itcommon
human_pr_text:it_279C Codex suggested itspecial, but with low confidence

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret resplit match reports by source and destination units together
accepted_candidate_records.text: A resplit can show many broken matches in the old unit while adding corresponding matches in new destination units; reviewers should evaluate aggregate moved ownership and progress instead of treating old-unit breakage alone as a regression.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:splits.txt split from it_2725 into new TUs

source_update_candidates.title: Item TU split ownership after PR 2546
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2546 split the old src/melee/it/it_2725.c address range into src/melee/it/it_2725.c, ithitbox.c, itmaplib.c, itmaterial.c, iteffect.c, itanimlist.c, it_279C.c, and itzako.c, with split ranges shown in config/GALE01/splits.txt.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:resplit it_2725 into a bunch of TUs
source_update_candidates.reason: Curator may want path facts for the item split boundaries and newly introduced item TU roles.

source_update_candidates.title: itmaplib remained nonmatching immediately after the resplit
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2546, itmaplib.c was split out for item map collision behavior but was left NonMatching in configure.py; the PR body says all new split units were matching and linked except itmaplib.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:All of these are matching and linked except itmaplib
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful historical path fact for future itmaplib matching or cleanup work.

source_update_candidates.title: Use embedded filename/string evidence when proposing TU resplits
source_update_candidates.text: For large TU resplits, record the evidence used for each new unit, especially assert filenames, object names, diagnostic strings, and repeated literal clusters. Treat repeated floats/strings as hints, not definitive source ownership on their own.
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides a concrete example of documenting mixed-strength evidence for TU boundary decisions; curator should decide whether this deserves a global standard or just a lesson.

rejection_notes: Do not promote it_2725 as itcommon or it_279C as itspecial; the PR body explicitly marks those AI-suggested names as unclear or low confidence.
Do not claim itmaplib was fully matching; the PR body and configure.py both identify it as the exception.
Do not infer exact semantic meanings for every moved function from the truncated diff; only the PR body's TU-level descriptions are supported here.
Do not treat bot report old-unit broken matches as standalone regressions without the resplit context; the same report lists destination-unit new matches.
